query: I have about 12,000 labeled chest X-ray images in my eye-ai catalog and I need to create a training dataset, split it 80/20, and make sure the test set has ground truth labels
my colleague sent me dataset RID 2-B4C8 and I want to understand what's in it before I plug it into my training config — can you help me explore the contents?
I just added 500 new OCT scans to the catalog and fixed some mislabeled images. My experiment config still points to dataset version 0.3.0. What do I need to do?
we're setting up a new project and need to figure out what dataset types to create. We have fundus and OCT images, some labeled by experts and some by a model, from two clinical sites
I want to see what's actually in dataset 3-XYZ9 — like what kinds of images are there, how the diagnoses break down, and whether any are missing labels
how do I reference dataset 28EA version 0.4.0 in my hydra-zen config file so the experiment picks it up?
I need a small dev subset of 500 images from the main dataset for quick iteration. Don't want to mess up the original
my BDBag download for the image dataset keeps timing out after about 10 minutes. The FK chain goes through Sample, Subject, Study, and Institution tables
I need to add Image as an element type so I can put images into datasets, and then check what element types are already registered in the catalog
what's the difference between a Complete dataset and a Split dataset? when should I use add_dataset_child vs split_dataset?
I need to create a new feature called Diagnosis that links Image records to a controlled vocabulary of diagnosis terms like Normal and Abnormal
can you help me write a hydra-zen config for my ResNet50 training run? I need to set up the model architecture, learning rate, and batch size parameters
I want to create a new table called Subject with columns for Name, Age, and Gender, plus a foreign key to the Study table
how do I run my training notebook with papermill and track the execution in the catalog? I want to use deriva-ml-run with my cifar10 experiment
I need to upload my trained model weights as an asset to the catalog within an execution context for provenance tracking
can you help me set up the Chaise display annotations so the Image table shows the filename and subject columns in the compact view?
I want to add a new vocabulary term 'Severe' to the existing Diagnosis_Type vocabulary and also add 'mild' as a synonym for 'Mild'
how do I authenticate with the Deriva server? I'm getting a 401 error when trying to connect. Do I need to log in through Globus first?
I need to denormalize the Image and Subject tables to build a pandas DataFrame for my analysis — how do I join them through the foreign key?
can you check what version of deriva-ml is installed and whether I need to update? also make sure nbstripout is configured for my notebooks
